summary: Iter32 nav-audit + regression PASS. Backend pytest 15/15 PASS in 70s (login, auth/me, logout, leads create + CSV import, campaign create+approve-and-send+duplicate+boost, scraping/start, competitors create+scan, ai/generate-content, quick-plan/generate, assistant/chat via Gemini, autopilot/kickoff). Frontend nav audit: all 19 sidebar links (18 user + 1 admin) clicked successfully and route to their pages without 404 or error boundary. Campaigns, Approvals, Landing Pages, and Inbox all reachable. SMTP send to ekodecrux@gmail.com confirmed via campaign reaching SENT status with sent_count>=1.

issues: React hydration warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' from a NavLink/select somewhere in AppLayout. Non-blocking but should be cleaned up — likely a <span> inside a native <select><option> tag in the sidebar/group rendering.

action_items: (Optional) Fix React hydration warning '<span> cannot be a child of <option>' surfaced in console (search for <option> tags wrapping <span> children in components rendered by AppLayout — possibly a Select inside a sidebar dropdown).
(Carry-over from iter26) Optional: server.py is >7800 lines; split into modules.
(Carry-over) Rename internal _llm_chat helper docs/comments are now consistent — confirmed via assistant/chat returning a non-empty Gemini reply.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js has duplicate route definitions for /landing-pages and /landing-pages/:id (lines 96-97 AND 101-102). React Router uses the first match so this isn't a runtime bug, but it's dead code that should be deduped.

AppLayout.jsx 4-group nav (Home/Grow/Engage/Settings) cleanly includes Campaigns under Engage with PaperPlaneTilt icon and dedups Approvals into Engage. All 18 user-facing routes have matching sidebar entries with data-testid='nav-*'.

All 13 critical POST endpoints from the request return 200/202 with valid payloads. /api/campaigns/{id}/approve-and-send fires real email through SMTP and the campaign correctly transitions PENDING_APPROVAL -> SENT with sent_count>=1.
/api/assistant/chat (Gemini 3 Flash via _llm_chat after rename) returns non-empty replies — LLM routing intact.
/api/auth/logout is cookie-only — bearer token in Authorization header still grants access to /api/auth/me afterward. This is documented behavior and matches the request expectation.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (19/19 nav links route correctly without errors)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_iter32_* leads (1 via API + 3 via CSV import), 1 TEST_iter32 campaign (sent to ekodecrux@gmail.com), 1 duplicate, 1 TEST_iter32_* competitor (stripe.com). All prefixed for cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Reusable regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iter32_regression.py covers 15 critical paths in ~70s. For schema reference: /api/campaigns POST requires {type, channel, content, subject, recipient_scope, ...} and returns {campaign: {...}}; /api/competitors POST requires {url, nickname} returns {competitor: {...}}; /api/scraping/start uses {type:'GOOGLE_MAPS_LEADS', location, keyword}; /api/quick-plan/generate uses {monthly_budget, duration_months}; /api/autopilot/kickoff requires {monthly_lead_target, avg_deal_value_usd}. /api/auth/me and /api/leads return nested {user:{}}/{lead:{}} shape. Login redirects admin to /admin — for AppLayout testing navigate to /dashboard manually after login.
rca of the issue: n/a — no bugs found in iter32. Iter25 regressions (missing Campaigns nav, missing /landing-pages route, dup /approvals) have been correctly fixed in iter32 per the source files. Hydration warning in console is cosmetic only.
